author: judge-correctness-c2

dimensionScores.rationale: Three old P1s resolved but the ~2000 LOC Phase-2 code shipped two NEW P1s that silently disable headline features on my lens: reconcile.py:56/65 seeds merged=scraped.copy() and skips audited-only periods, truncating FSDS deep history to yfinance's ~4y window for every scraped US large-cap; companies_house.py:197-198 digits[-1] picks the trailing yyyymmdd date not the company number, so the UK layer ingests zero rows. Plus a broad correctness tail (F8 stale import-prices, F12 single-spot FX across all periods, F13/F14 EDINET interim/parent-only, F16 BOM zeroes the GLEIF map) — net flat-to-down vs 3.7.

216 pytest + 62 vitest all green (runs/pytest.txt, vitest.txt) with the F3 PerformanceWarning gone, but the suite has demonstrable blind spots that let both P1s through: tests/test_companies_house.py:62 uses a single-digit-run fixture so digits[-1]==digits[0] and masks F7; no fixture covers an audited period outside the scraped index (F6), a co-registrant row (F10), or a BOM'd GLEIF CSV (F16). Green but false-confidence on the new sources.

Essentially flat vs 3.97. Only new issue is F5 — mirror.py:93-95 streams to disk with no byte ceiling and mirror.py:80 uses a per-op (not total) timeout, a disk-fill/slow-loris DoS on the ~200MB GLEIF auto-heal path; URLs are hardcoded/trusted (gleif.py:21, fx.py:26) so no SSRF, keyless design intact.

The F4 refactor genuinely halved service.py (820->523 LOC, verified 523) via the AuditedBulkProvider seam, but the extracted cycles all landed in enrichment.py which is now the #2 hotspot at 617 LOC nesting 14 with 9 parallel run_* cycles (F1), and mirror.py:98 writes the meta sidecar non-atomically (F2). Slight net improvement over 3.43 — complexity was relocated, not dissolved.

F3 fragmentation fixed — ranks.py:98-106 does one pd.concat and pytest shows no PerformanceWarning — but F11 introduces a fresh OOM-class regression on the self-hosted target: gleif.py:43/47 triple-buffers the whole ISIN-LEI zip (~1GB decompressed) and edgar_fsds.py reads num.txt whole into RAM, the same defect class as the prior-cycle F13 the mirror streaming was meant to prevent. Roughly flat vs 4.07.

topFindings: F6 (P1, correctness): reconcile.py:56/65 seeds merged from the scrape and skips audited-only periods, so FSDS/EDGAR deep history is truncated to yfinance's ~4y window for every scraped symbol — the headline Phase-2 deep-history feature is silently inert for US large/mid caps.

F7 (P1, correctness): companies_house.py:197-198 re.findall+digits[-1] returns the trailing yyyymmdd filing date, not the company number, on the real Prod<nnn>_<batch>_<number>_<date> filenames — the UK audited layer ingests zero rows and 'succeeds' silently; masked by the single-digit-run unit fixture at tests/test_companies_house.py:62.

Correctness tail: F16 utf-8 (not utf-8-sig) decode zeroes the whole GLEIF map on a BOM'd file; F12 applies one latest FX spot rate across all fiscal periods; F13/F14 book EDINET interim/parent-only figures as annual/consolidated; F8/F15 leave incremental compute blind to price-dump refresh and newest-raw deletion.
